mission.title: Install and Configure MicroK8s
mission.description: MicroK8s is a lightweight, single-package Kubernetes distribution designed for simplicity and speed, ideal for development environments, IoT, and edge computing. It provides a fully conformant Kubernetes experience with minimal overhead, making it perfect for local development or CI/CD pipelines. However, it is primarily suited for single-node deployments and may not be ideal for large-scale production environments.

steps.title: Install MicroK8s
steps.description:
This step installs MicroK8s using Snap, which is the recommended method for installation.

```bash

sudo snap install microk8s --classic

```

> Installs the latest version of MicroK8s.

steps.title: Add User to MicroK8s Group
steps.description:
This step allows your user to run MicroK8s commands without sudo by adding it to the microk8s group.

```bash

sudo usermod -a -G microk8s $USER

newgrp microk8s

```

> Replace $USER with your username if needed.
> Refresh group membership in the current session.

steps.title: Enable Essential Add-ons
steps.description:
This step enables essential add-ons like DNS and the Kubernetes dashboard for better functionality.

```bash

sudo microk8s enable dns

sudo microk8s enable dashboard

```

> Enables DNS for service discovery.
> Enables the Kubernetes dashboard for UI access.

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the MicroK8s installation and verifies that the nodes are up and running.

```bash

microk8s status

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> Displays the status of MicroK8s and enabled add-ons.
> Lists the nodes in the cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MicroK8s
uninstall.description:
This step removes MicroK8s from your system. Be aware that all Kubernetes resources will be lost.

```bash

sudo snap remove microk8s

```

> This command will remove MicroK8s and all associated data.

uninstall.title: Remove User from MicroK8s Group
uninstall.description:
This step removes your user from the microk8s group to prevent access to MicroK8s commands.

```bash

sudo gpasswd -d $USER microk8s

```

> Replace $USER with your username if needed.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MicroK8s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your resources. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the installation.

```bash

microk8s stop

sudo snap refresh microk8s

microk8s start

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> Stops MicroK8s to prepare for the upgrade.
> Upgrades MicroK8s to the latest version.
> Starts MicroK8s after the upgrade.
> Verifies that the nodes are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'microk8s: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s is not installed or the snap path is not set.

**Fix:**
Install MicroK8s using 'sudo snap install microk8s --classic'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s is not started or has crashed.

**Fix:**
Run 'microk8s start' to start the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command is not recognized.

**Fix:**
Use 'microk8s kubectl' instead of 'kubectl'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo microk8s kubectl config view --raw > $HOME/.kube/config' to set up kubeconfig.

versionNotes.changes: Updated to Kubernetes v1.31, added support for new APIs and features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs that are no longer supported.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to check for deprecated APIs in your manifests.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'microk8s kubectl get nodes' showing your node(s) in a 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a compatible Linux distribution with Snap installed.
